title: Trump I EPA holds PFAS National Leadership Summit and community engagement tour
description: EPA held a two-day National Leadership Summit in Washington, D.C. (July 10–11, 2018) bringing together federal, state, and local officials, advocates, and affected communities. Followed by a six-city community engagement tour (New England, Pennsylvania, Colorado, North Carolina, Heartland, and a correction notice for Pennsylvania). The summit and tour produced the political momentum that led to the 2019 PFAS Action Plan. All six engagement meetings were logged as Federal Register notices under docket EPA-HQ-OW-2018-0270.

title: 5th Circuit vacates EPA's TSCA Section 5 orders against Inhance Technologies
description: In Inhance Technologies v. EPA (No. 23-60620), the Fifth Circuit ruled EPA exceeded its TSCA Section 5 authority by issuing 'significant new use' orders to ban PFAS generation in a 40-year-old plastics fluorination process. The court vacated the orders, directing EPA to instead use Section 6 rulemaking. Sets a precedent limiting EPA's fastest PFAS enforcement tool under TSCA.

title: Biden EPA finalizes PFAS drinking water MCL rule
description: Sets legally enforceable limits: 4 ppt for PFOA and PFOS; 10 ppt for PFNA, PFHxS, and GenX (HFPO-DA); and a Hazard Index for PFBS mixtures. Affects water systems serving roughly 66 million Americans. Compliance deadline: April 2029.

title: AWWA and AMWA petition D.C. Circuit to challenge Biden MCL rule
description: The American Water Works Association (AWWA) and Association of Metropolitan Water Agencies (AMWA) filed a petition for review in the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit (No. 24-1188), challenging EPA's PFAS drinking water rule as arbitrary and capricious and exceeding the Safe Drinking Water Act's requirements. Additional industry petitions followed. The D.C. Circuit consolidated the challenges.

title: Biden EPA designates PFOA and PFOS as CERCLA hazardous substances
description: Effective July 8, 2024. First use of CERCLA hazardous substance authority for PFAS. Triggers mandatory cleanup reporting and Superfund liability for releases. Industry challenges filed immediately in the D.C. Circuit.

title: Trump administration takes office — PFAS regulation under review
description: Trump EPA begins review of Biden-era PFAS regulations. Administrator Lee Zeldin takes over. The MAHA (Make America Healthy Again) agenda, led by HHS Secretary RFK Jr., creates internal pressure to address environmental toxins including PFAS — in direct tension with industry-aligned deregulatory priorities.

title: Trump EPA withdraws Biden-era effluent limits for PFAS (ELG rule)
description: On Day 2 of the new administration, EPA withdrew the effluent limitations guidelines (ELG) that would have required industrial facilities to limit PFAS discharges into waterways. The rule had not yet taken effect. Withdrawal required no public comment period.

title: D.C. Circuit denies EPA's motion to vacate PFAS MCLs
description: The D.C. Circuit Court of Appeals denied EPA's request to vacate the Biden PFAS drinking water MCLs pending reconsideration — meaning the rules remained in effect. The court found EPA had not shown vacatur was warranted. This was a critical legal setback for rollback efforts. Case: No. 24-1188 (consolidated).

title: D.C. Circuit grants 60-day stay of MCL litigation at EPA's request
description: The D.C. Circuit granted a 60-day administrative stay in AWWA v. EPA (No. 24-1188), giving the Trump EPA time to reconsider the Biden MCL rule. Extended by 30 days on April 10, 2025. The stay paused merits briefing but did not lift the MCLs themselves — water systems remained bound by the rules.

title: Zeldin announces 'major EPA actions to combat PFAS contamination'
description: Press release touts EPA leadership on PFAS. Announces PFAS-OUT initiative. Language emphasizes cleanup and accountability — framed as pro-public health. Does not mention ongoing reconsideration of MCLs for GenX, PFHxS, PFNA, and PFBS.

title: Trump EPA announces partial MCL rollback — rescinds limits for 4 compounds
description: EPA retains 4 ppt MCLs for PFOA and PFOS but rescinds the limits for GenX, PFHxS, PFNA, and PFBS. Compliance deadline extended from 2029 to 2031. Justification: that rulemaking for those four compounds did not properly follow SDWA's 'regulatory determination' process. Earthjustice and state AGs challenge the move in court.

title: Earthjustice and state AGs challenge Trump MCL rollback in D.C. Circuit
description: Earthjustice, joined by multiple state attorneys general, filed a petition for review challenging EPA's partial rescission of the four PFAS drinking water MCLs (GenX, PFHxS, PFNA, PFBS). Consolidated with the existing AWWA v. EPA docket (No. 24-1188). Arguments split: industry petitioners sought to overturn all six MCLs; Earthjustice argues the four rescissions were unlawful.

title: MAHA Commission releases report — names PFAS as chronic-disease driver
description: The Make America Healthy Again Commission (chaired by RFK Jr.) releases its health report, citing PFAS exposure alongside pesticides and food dyes as contributors to chronic disease in children. Published roughly two weeks after EPA's MCL rollback announcement — creating a visible contradiction within the administration.

title: Trump EPA retains CERCLA designation for PFOA/PFOS
description: Surprising continuity move: EPA announces it will keep the CERCLA hazardous substance designation and defend it in court. Commits to 'holding polluters accountable.' Announces enforcement discretion for water utilities, municipalities, farmers, landfills, and fire departments — focusing liability on manufacturers.

title: Trump EPA proposes TSCA reporting exemptions — reduces PFAS data requirements
description: Proposes de minimis exemption (below 0.1% concentration), imported articles exemption, and expanded byproduct/impurity exemptions. Estimated to save industry 10–11 million reporting hours and $786–843 million. Comment period closed December 29, 2025. Final rule expected 2026.

title: D.C. Circuit denies EPA's stay motion on PFAS MCL rescissions
description: The D.C. Circuit denied EPA's motion to stay enforcement of the MCL rule while the partial rescission is litigated. The four rescinded MCLs (GenX, PFHxS, PFNA, PFBS) are being challenged by Earthjustice and state attorneys general. Full merits argument expected fall 2026.
notes: TODO: Verify date and citation when court order is published.

title: EPA launches PFAS OUT initiative — proactive monitoring framework
description: EPA announces the PFAS OUT (Overseeing Unregulated Treatment) Initiative, designed to proactively identify PFAS contamination in drinking water systems before it reaches unsafe levels. Billed as a prevention-first approach supplementing the retained PFOA/PFOS MCLs.

title: Trump EPA updates PFAS destruction and disposal guidance
description: EPA releases updated guidance on how to safely destroy or dispose of PFAS-containing materials. Expands approved thermal treatment methods. Addresses disposal of AFFF (aqueous film-forming foam) and other PFAS waste streams.
